The Bizarre Email Marketing “Love Letter”
That Made Me $10,000,000
Stop emailing a list. Start talking to one person.
The businesses who will generate both profit and impact in 2019 are the
ones who understand the difference.
When you get stuck in the “blast to your list” mentality, sending out
boilerplate emails or swipes on a weekly basis, you may make money.
However, you will never build a brand. Inspire trust. Compel the receiver to
forward your email and grow your list virally.
You will never see your prospect fall in love with you, your business, or your
message.
I got this wrong for most of my email marketing career. I still mess it up
today unless I use the tools I created to keep me in check. It’s all too easy
to fall into lazy email practices. After all, email has a 38-1 return on
investment. It’s the fastest way into your prospect’s world.
That kind of power can make you lazy.
I’ll share more about my own laziness in a moment, and how it led to a
breakthrough of over $10,000,000 in email marketing revenue.
What changed?
It all started when I realized something so obvious in hindsight that it now
sounds almost pedestrian:
You don’t have a list. You have “one person” residing within a list of other
“one persons.” And you absolutely must speak to everyone using one-person
language patterns.
One person. That’s all you ‘ever’ write to. Every email, no matter how hard
you’re selling, must contain three specific elements to pull this off. Elements
that create the one-person impact we’re looking for.
It just so happens to be that these elements are found in virtually every love
letter ever written. They are foundational to love itself.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 6 --

Stay with me.
Everyone on your list is like a penpal. Someone who wants to know you, and
be known. To be heard and understood. Who loves and fears. Who hurts and
needs relief.
Who trusts you enough to share a few moments of their life.
Consider this:
You have about 27,000 days to relish the pleasure of existence. From the
time you draw your first breath until your very last.
The number seems large until you think about it. How fast did the last 365
of your days go by? And remember, you sleep for ⅓ of this time.
This adds up quickly. So much so that the most common complaint older
folks have is "how fast the time goes."
What do they know that you may have forgotten?
And how did you spend your days and minutes yesterday?
Living optimistically? Loving the moment? Relishing the journey? Existing in
that epic state of flow and independence?
Every second counts.
However, if you’re like most people, those seconds may have been wasted
laboring between the dramas of your present life and the expectations of the
life you imagine.
Those names and emails that reside within your list are no different than
you. And they are looking for answers.
They give you a share of their moments for those answers. What you give
back to them should reflect that choice.
So now you have the opportunity to send each of them a love letter.
Every day. Even twice a day.
What do I mean?
Realize this isn’t a romantic gesture. This isn’t gushy. Or sentimental. Or
embarrassing. I mail in the fitness, bodybuilding, and martial arts spaces
frequently. This can be as “alpha” as you desire.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 6 --

This doesn’t require any list segmentation. No new tech tricks. No funnels.
No gimmicks. Just a few choice words and an attitude.
Take on this, and watch your business grow in extraordinary ways.
As I mentioned earlier, I was a pretty lousy email lover for much of my
marketing career. Wisdom is being smart enough to heed your own advice,
and I was far from Solomon in the wisdom department.
Like many successful entrepreneurs, I got caught up in the idea of list
management. Segmentation. Optimization. EPCs and conversions. Funnels
and autoresponder sequences.
Important stuff, but hardly pillow talk.
I was always in a hurry. “Get me a swipe and I’ll send it.” “Whatever
converts.” Even when it worked it felt empty. And I knew I wasn’t really
impacting all those penpals who loaned me their precious seconds and
minutes of life.
I struggled for years to figure out why some emails worked fairly well, yet
left my buyers feeling uninspired. Others converted like crazy and turned my
list into raving fans. The words seemed the same.
Were there subtle distinctions that made all the difference?
It turns out there were three.
Three powerful elements that turn ordinary sales and content emails into
love letter emails.
Three character traits of love itself that, when infused into any promotional
or content email, turns them into lighten rods of persuasion and impact.
Three elements that serve as a pretty good definition of love itself:
“Love is service, rendered with empathy and hope.”
Service. Empathy. Hope.
How can you serve your reader? What do you have that will take them from
a point of pain to a point of pleasure? How can you empathize with them?
Speak to them in one-to-one language patterns? Are you directing them
toward hope and away from chaos?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 6 --

This revelation was so potent I decided to code the words and phrases that
convey these elements into an online machine so I wouldn’t forget to use
them. Now Email CopyPro helps thousands of marketers sell more and
create a greater impact on their customers.
To be honest, hardly anyone even knows these love letter elements are even
in the mix. My customers just think it’s awesome persuasion copy. And it is.
They just don’t know why.
Let’s cover each of these elements in detail.
1. Love as Service
Love is not an emotion. It is an action that creates emotion as a byproduct.
My mother and father were epic loves. They met and married within three
weeks, and were married until the day my father died in his sleep at 83.
For ten years, my mom never took her ring off. She was proposed to twice.
Each time the reply was the same: “I’m sorry, but I’m married.”
That love was forged from the iron of The Greatest Generation, and while
there was a lot of emotions, action ruled the house.
Love is a verb.
When you go to love your penpals you first start with acts of service. Give
them ideas. Give them gifts. Give them products and services that ease pain
and render pleasure.
Give them access to you in ways that they won’t believe. I’ll occasionally call
the folks who buy from me. They know it’s my voice because they’ve heard
me speak on video prior to buying. The impact that makes is hard to
describe.
How can you serve your penpals today? Even saying, “I’m committed to
serving you” carries the impact of a love letter. Notice how, “We are
committed to serving you” loses a bit of that impact.
Why?
Because love, in most cases, is a one-to-one phenomenon. That’s why you
should always write as if you’re speaking to one person. Always. Even if you
are writing as a company or a partnership and have to use “we”, consider,
“I’m here to serve you, and so is ____.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 6 --

Little distinctions create the biggest results.
2. Love as Empathy
To empathize is to seek to understand. To recall similar moments of
weakness. To relive your own struggles as you teach and inspire.
Empathy was a revered quality among the Greeks. Demeter was a goddess
known for her empathy and loved for her compassion for the human
condition. So much so that she is the Goddess of the Bountiful Harvest.
Empathy is the cornerstone of bounty. It transforms being rich into living
wealthy. It is the most important character trait when it comes to ethical
persuasion.
Just consider these words:
“I understand.”
“I feel you’re hurting.”
“I relate to that.”
“It’s not your fault.”
These are all empathetic phrases used frequently in some of the most
powerfully persuasive copy. When taken out of context as I have here, the
energy and nature of the words leap off the page.
Lead with empathetic language and you can follow with just about any level
of instruction. Once someone feels understood, they begin to trust. And trust
is the beginning of a profitable and impactful transaction.
3. Love as Hope
Hope springs eternal. However, hope often receives a bad rap, primarily due
to unethical marketers selling hope alone.
Selling nothing but hope is selling an illusion. You may pay the bills but you
won’t sleep well in the bed they buy.
Selling solutions infused with hope – with a vision of your customer that’s
greater and more inspiring than they dared to envision – is selling with love.
By definition, hope is future-paced. Your job is to paint a picture of how life
will be after they take you up on your offer, and do so as if it has already
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 6 --

happened. Be realistic, yet optimistic. Give your penpal the gift of seeing
and experiencing life as it truly can be.
They are one decision away from the hope of a better life.
Lastly, keep this in mind:
Sterile marketers generate money. Conscious marketers create legacies.
Impact lives. Grow exponentially thanks to real results shared by fans and
readers who feel heard, loved and understood.
I want you to be ruthlessly yet ethically persuasive. Sell hard. Sell often.
Just do it with the three elements of the email love letter in mind, at least
most of the time.
Do that and you’ll see monumental growth in every metric that counts.
— Jon Benson
